title: .NET interop: server-tier CLR call with InteropPermission and a real CLRError diagnostic
instruction: In the sandbox model, create a class DemoInteropCsv that calls .NET correctly. Ground it first with get_knowledge(topic="dotnet-interop"). Requirements: the file declares `using System.Text;` above the class declaration; the single public method is `public static server str buildLine(container _values)`; it asserts `new InteropPermission(InteropKind::ClrInterop).assert();` before touching CLR types and reverts the assert in a finally block with CodeAccessPermission::revertAssert(); it builds the comma-separated line with a System.Text.StringBuilder (Append/ToString), not with string concatenation in a loop; and it catches CLR failures with `catch (Exception::CLRError)`, pulls the message out of CLRInterop::getLastException() and surfaces it through error() with a label-based strFmt. A bare `catch (Exception::Error)` around the CLR call FAILS this case - it does not catch a CLR exception and the diagnostic is lost. The class must build with zero BP errors.
